title: Dataset v4 extended pilot with control anchor
description: Extension of the v4 pilot adding control (baseline-only) runs plus more compact-a/compact-b coverage. Designed for a 2-3 day unattended VM collection with retry-resilient export and provenance recording.
development_stage: v4 extended pilot for ML and benchmark research

collection_strategy.why_batch_collection: Control runs first anchor the false-positive distribution. Compact-a and compact-b runs follow with alternation to preserve corpus balance under early termination.

plans.purpose: Pure baseline runs (no faults). Anchor false-positive distribution and supply additional noise-class training data.
Latency, dependency outage, restart, Redis degradation, bad configuration, and near-miss coverage.
Service-diverse outages, frontend and Redis restarts, intermittent Redis failure, and noisy traffic coverage.

benchmark_tracks.description: Primary task: classify each telemetry window as ticket_worthy / borderline / noise.
Combined task: classification grounded in Jira memory corpus retrieval. Requires triage_evidence_text.
Secondary task: given a Jira issue, rank candidate telemetry episodes.

promotion_gates.raw_runs: Every collected run must pass validate-dataset-run.ps1 with 0 errors and validate-run-feature-distribution.py with 0 anomalies flagged.
promotion_gates.derived_outputs: Per-run triage_examples.jsonl must populate all FEATURE_COLUMNS with >=1 non-zero value across the run, and label distribution must include ticket_worthy + borderline + noise (control runs are exempt for ticket_worthy + borderline).
promotion_gates.aggregate_outputs: Global triage dataset must include all 13 scenario families across runs.
